criteria_results.title: Allegation 1: Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations â€” Identified

The output references 'Allegation Category 1 (Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations)' multiple times, cites the specific statistical evidence (t-statistic of 4.73, p<0.001), identifies the relevant statutory violations (Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act), and names the responsible parties (Whitmore and Chao). The allegation is described in detail across multiple sections of the output.

criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output extensively discusses Sections 206(1) and 206(2) of the Advisers Act, noting that both provisions are alleged to have been violated by cherry-picking, misleading Form ADV disclosures, and directed brokerage. The output mentions scienter in other contexts (e.g., Whitmore's awareness of compliance obligations), but never in connection with the 206(1)/206(2) distinction itself.

criteria_results.title: ISSUE_009: Omnibus Trade Percentage Discrepancy (73% vs. 78%)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not identify the discrepancy between the 73% figure (5,776/7,912) stated in the referral notice and the 78% figure stated in the statistical exhibit. While both figures appear in the output (73% appears in the cherry-picking section and 78% appears in the Whitmore section referencing the quantitative analysis), the agent never flags these as inconsistent or contradictory. The output treats them as separate facts without noting the conflict between them.

criteria_results.title: ISSUE_009: Explanation of Why Omnibus Percentage Discrepancy Matters
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions both the 73% figure (e.g., 'representing approximately 73% of total equity trading volume') and the 78% figure (e.g., 'These 5,776 omnibus trades represented approximately 78% of WCA's total equity trading volume during the Review Period') in different bullet points, but never explicitly identifies these as a discrepancy, let alone explains why the discrepancy matters. There is no discussion of implications such as different denominators, unreliable underlying data, or how the defense could use this inconsistency to challenge the SEC's statistical methodology. The two figures simply appear in separate bullet points without any connection or analysis.

criteria_results.title: ISSUE_010: WCA's Respondeat Superior / Failure to Supervise Liability
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output names WCA as a respondent across all six allegation categories and notes that 'WCA bears primary responsibility for compliance with the Advisers Act and the rules promulgated thereunder, including responsibility for the conduct of its associated persons acting within the scope of their employment.' However, the output does not specifically flag respondeat superior or control person liability for Bellini's directed brokerage conduct, nor does it identify potential 'failure to supervise' exposure under Section 203(e)(6) of the Investment Advisers Act. The output discusses Bellini's directed brokerage arrangement with Lakeshore Trading and WCA's failure to disclose it in Form ADV, but never connects WCA's entity-level liability specifically to Bellini's conduct through a respondeat superior or failure-to-supervise theory. The compliance review failures (Hargrove & Tilton not detecting the arrangement, best execution review gaps) are mentioned but not linked to a Section 203(e)(6) failure-to-supervise charge. The criterion requires more than merely naming WCA as a respondent — it requires flagging the specific legal theory of entity liability for Bellini's conduct, which the output does not do.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's Exposure: Cherry-Picking Allegation (12% LP Interest)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output clearly identifies Whitmore's personal exposure to the cherry-picking allegation due to his 12% LP interest in Oceanic Fund I LP. Under the 'Gerald R. Whitmore received preferential trade allocations' section, the output explicitly states that Whitmore 'holds a 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP valued at approximately $22.2 million as of December 31, 2023, creating a financial incentive for preferential allocation to that vehicle.' Additionally, paragraph 81 in the output lists Whitmore as implicated in 'Allegation Category 1 (Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations, as the individual who benefited through his 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP and who, as CEO and CIO, exercised oversight over allocation practices).' The output also includes paragraph 20 explaining how superior performance in Oceanic Fund I LP directly increases the value of Whitmore's LP interest, creating overlapping financial incentives.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's Exposure: Form ADV Misrepresentation
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly addresses Whitmore's personal exposure to the Form ADV misrepresentation allegation. In the section on Gerald R. Whitmore, paragraph 81 states that 'Whitmore is implicated in the following allegation categories: Allegation Category 1 (Cherry-Picking Trade Allocations...) Allegation Category 2 (Misleading Form ADV Disclosures, as the CEO and individual ultimately responsible for the accuracy of the Firm's regulatory filings)...' The output also notes that WCA's Form ADV 'failed to disclose that Whitmore holds a 12% personal limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP' and references Section 207 of the Advisers Act violations related to misleading Form ADV disclosures. The output clearly identifies Whitmore's personal exposure to the Form ADV misrepresentation allegation.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's Exposure: Obstruction Allegation (File Deletion)
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly addresses Whitmore's personal exposure to the obstruction allegation for instructing file deletion in multiple places. Most directly, paragraph 81 states that Whitmore is implicated in 'Allegation Category 6 (Obstructive Conduct During Examination, as the individual who directly instructed the deletion of trade allocation records).' Additionally, paragraph 62 describes the specific conduct: 'On or about April 22, 2024, during the pendency of the SEC examination...Whitmore instructed WCA's IT Administrator, Tomás Delgado, to "clean up the shared drive" by deleting certain folders...that contained trade allocation spreadsheets.' Paragraph 67 further notes this constitutes 'a clear violation of Section 209(e),' and paragraph 75 explicitly ties Section 209(e) to Whitmore's instruction to delete records. The output clearly identifies Whitmore's personal exposure to the obstruction allegation.

criteria_results.title: Individuals at Risk: Chao's Personal Exposure Summarized
criteria_results.reasoning: While the agent's output does address Chao's involvement in the cherry-picking allegation and mentions her personal brokerage account (Account No. ending -7823) with an 81.6% profitable allocation rate (71 out of 87 allocations), it does not summarize her personal exposure in a consolidated manner under the 'Individuals at Risk and Personal Exposure' section. That section contains only a generic statement about safe harbor provisions and a boilerplate disclaimer about the referral document. The specific ~$438K excess profits figure for Chao's personal account is never mentioned anywhere in the output. The criterion requires identification of the 81.6% profitable allocation rate AND the ~$438K excess profits figure for her personal account. The $438K figure is entirely absent from the output, making this a fail.

criteria_results.title: Individuals at Risk: Bellini's Personal Exposure Summarized
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output does not adequately summarize Bellini's personal exposure for the directed brokerage/kickback scheme. While the output mentions Bellini in connection with directed brokerage to Lakeshore Trading, the $291,400 in excess commissions, and the relationship with Kevin Ostrowski (40% owner of Lakeshore Trading), it fails to include the undisclosed personal compensation figure of $92,600 per referral. The 'Individuals at Risk and Personal Exposure' section for Bellini is essentially empty of substantive content. The 'Key figures' line under Bellini's section only states 'Marcus T. Bellini --- Bellini is implicated in Allegation: 40%' which is incomplete and cryptic. The criterion specifically requires summarization of Bellini's relationship with Ostrowski, the $291,400 excess commissions, AND the $92,600 undisclosed personal compensation - the last element is entirely absent from the output.

criteria_results.title: Total Alleged Ill-Gotten Gains / Client Harm Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly states the total alleged ill-gotten gains figure of $8,622,000 multiple times throughout the document. For example, it states 'The SEC's total alleged ill-gotten gains figure of $8,622,000 exceeds the Oceanic Fund I cherry-picking figure by approximately $822,000' and 'Total alleged ill-gotten gains and client harm of $8,622,000 --- exceeding the Oceanic Fund I figure by approximately $822,000.' The output also notes the $7,800,000 cherry-picking component and the $822,000 differential representing other harm categories. While the output does not explicitly break down the $822,000 into the $291,400 excess commissions and $92,600 undisclosed compensation sub-components, it does provide the aggregate $8,622,000 figure, which satisfies the criterion.

criteria_results.title: Hargrove & Tilton Review: Failure to Test Trade Allocation Noted
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly mentions that the Hargrove & Tilton annual compliance review (dated December 8, 2023) did not test trade allocation patterns despite the firm's heavy reliance on omnibus trading. This is captured in paragraph 39 quoted in the output: 'The annual compliance review conducted by Hargrove & Tilton LLP for the calendar year 2023, dated December 8, 2023, did not test trade allocation patterns despite the Firm's heavy reliance on omnibus trading.' The output also notes that H&T identified only minor procedural deficiencies and that the Division alleges WCA's reliance on the H&T review does not satisfy its independent obligation under Rule 206(4)-7, identifying this as relevant to WCA's deficient compliance infrastructure.

criteria_results.title: Key Dates: Examination Timeline Accurately Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output only explicitly states one of the five required dates: the examination commenced March 11, 2024 (mentioned in paragraph 62 context and in the 'Key Dates and Examination Timeline' section as 'March 11, 2024 to August 2, 2024 Examination Conduct'). August 2, 2024 appears as an endpoint reference in the timeline section. However, the referral to Enforcement date (October 18, 2024), the referral notice date (November 6, 2024), and the service date (November 12, 2024) are not stated anywhere in the output. With only two of the five required dates present (March 11, 2024 and August 2, 2024), the output fails to meet the threshold of at least three correctly stated dates.

criteria_results.title: Whitmore's 12% LP Interest Value Correctly Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly states Whitmore's 12% limited partnership interest in Oceanic Fund I LP was valued at approximately $22.2 million as of December 31, 2023, and also references the calculation basis of 12% × $185M (net assets of Oceanic Fund I LP) in multiple places throughout the document.

criteria_results.title: Compliance Manual Last Updated November 15, 2022
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions 'which was last updated on November 15, 2022' multiple times, including in the 'Key Dates and Examination Timeline' section and in the 'WCA breached its fiduciary duty to its other advisory clients' section, explicitly noting the Compliance Manual was last updated on November 15, 2022.

criteria_results.title: Lakeshore Trading LLC: Ostrowski's 40% Ownership Stated
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output explicitly states 'any relationship between WCA employee Bellini and Kevin Ostrowski (a 40% owner of Lakeshore Trading)' in the section about WCA engaging in a cherry-picking scheme. This correctly identifies both Kevin Ostrowski's identity and his 40% ownership stake in Lakeshore Trading LLC.

criteria_results.title: Lakeshore Trading LLC: Ostrowski-Bellini Personal Relationship and Undisclosed Nature
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output mentions the relationship between Bellini and Kevin Ostrowski (a 40% owner of Lakeshore Trading) and notes it was undisclosed in WCA's Form ADV, but never identifies the specific nature of the personal relationship — that Ostrowski is Bellini's college roommate. The criterion requires identification of this specific fact (college roommate relationship) to pass.

criteria_results.title: Email Exhibit Language Quoted or Paraphrased
criteria_results.reasoning: The agent's output references Whitmore's instruction to 'clean up the shared drive' (paragraph 62 is quoted) but does not reference the whitmore-delgado-email.eml exhibit specifically, nor does it note the ambiguous language as potentially supporting an innocent interpretation. The output treats the deletion instruction as straightforwardly obstructive rather than noting any ambiguity in the email's language that could support an innocent reading.
